title: pilosella paragoga subsp. paragogiceps
intro: Pilosella paragoga subsp. paragogiceps, also known as a subspecies of the knapweed plant, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pilosella paragoga subsp. paragogiceps include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
